title: Epicenter of Flavors Indian Chicken Vindaloo
description: A citrusy and snappy Indian dish that combines the flavors of chicken, spices, and citrus, cooked in a quick-fix manner using a pandan leaf wrap. This recipe is low in sodium and perfect for a weeknight meal.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the onions and cook until they are translucent and slightly caramelized, about 5 minutes.
Add the garam masala, turmeric, garam masala powder, and cayenne pepper to the pan and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken to the pan and cook until it is browned on all sides and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the lemon juice and ginger paste to the pan and stir to combine.
Season the chicken with salt and black pepper to taste.
Place the chicken in the center of four pandan leaves and wrap them around the chicken to form a neat package, tucking the ends under the chicken.
Heat a large non-stick pan or griddle over medium-high heat and add a small amount of oil to it.
Place the wrapped chicken packages in the pan and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until the pandan leaves are slightly charred and the chicken is cooked through.
Serve the chicken hot, garnished with lemon wedges and cilantro if desired.
